reviews.review_msg:
He is very disorganized.

If I were you, I would stay away from his courses and wait for another lecturer/prof to take this course instead.

Really nice prof when you get in contact with him, but you will have to either email/meet after class since he is basically absent from the discussion forums. Grading was very chill, you really only have to worry about the weekly exercises since those are the bulk of where the learning comes from, then a group project in the last week or two.

Him and his TAs almost never respond to emails. It's almost impossible to reach out to them on any issues, either grading, project proposal, or questions. Assignment and quiz grades are not released until weeks after the course ends. Seem to scale the class down for the final grade. Highly disappointed

Never responds to questions from students and explains concepts very poorly during lecture. He uses all of Greg Bakers materials to teach the course so just take the course with him or watch Greg's videos instead. He's a nice guy but he did the bare minimum.

There are some lecturers who are incompetent. 

There are some lecturers who are unprofessional. 

Mr. Bergner somehow has combined to be both.

The quality at the university has gone down drastically. 

There is no better evidence for this fact then this lecturer. 

You would be better off spending your time and money on the internet than his courses.

This is my only positive rating on this site. I personally view how this guy runs CMPT353 as the way every course in this school should be run. No forced attendance except quiz, material is applicable to the real world, grading is fair. Only issue was lack of communication online, but, it was not usually needed. You could always go to office hours.
Just reads from his slides. The class was rushed by the end of the term. Does not communicate well. No response to emails or discussion posts.
Youtube will do a better job than this.

He deserves all 5 stars for SURE. His courses taught me how to think, not just fumble around with code until it does something. Also, he's a lecturer, so he devotes himself to teaching (unlike other profs who do research). His lectures are easy to follow and the assignments are very fair. Bonus: he's slightly cynical and very funny.

He doesn't teach, he just talks for two hours. No clear grading criteria or outline for final project worth 22%. Nothing can prepare you for his multiple choice questions. Honestly, he's a rude person.

JUST AVOID HIM. Hands down the worst prof I've had at SFU. Rude with a terrible attitude, especially to international students. Streams lectures of him coughing every couple of sentences all semester long weekly on YouTube. Really hard to follow lectures. This was not what I signed up for in an in-person class. Such a waste of time and tuition.

Assignments are pretty easy to get 100% on (Greg would even point you in the right direction if asked). If you get 100% on them and understood what to do, you would likely get a high grade on the project. The quizzes are fairly straight forward if you memorized key points and did the assignments. 100% online with exception of quizzes. B average.

Well thought-out weekly assignments. His focus is on helping you learn rather than creating pointless difficulty. My only gripe is that some quiz MC qs felt arbitrary. I also would've liked more guidelines on the final project. Some confuse his sarcastic demeanour for rudeness, but he was always friendly in my experience. Definitely recommend Greg.

In CMPT 353, Professor Baker provides a clear, intuitive connection between probability, statistics, data science, machine learning, and cloud computing that is as enjoyable as it is easy to follow. Lectures are logical and the weekly coding assignments are reasonably demanding, enabling a deep understanding of the material.
He did a very good job teaching data science and the lectures were all well made. Highly recommend taking this course.

Great person, Great class
I loved how it wasnt hard to understand the lectures, but the assignments where hard tbh
